initial_draft:
<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason>Player BLUE has increased their offer to 43 ZUP. This is above my production cost and a reasonable profit. I'll accept this offer to finalize the trade.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>Great! I accept your offer of 43 ZUP for the X. Looking forward to the trade!</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and correctly ordered, with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 43 ZUP is a reasonable profit given the 40 ZUP production cost. No immediate utility gain possible without risking rejection.
3. Opponent plausibility: Given the opponent's willingness to increase their offer to 43 ZUP, accepting is highly plausible. Rejecting would likely end the game without a favorable outcome.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> aligns with the acceptance of the trade and the message reinforces the agreement.
5. Rule compliance: OK. We are within the proposal budget and respecting the turn constraints.
</critique>
